pages.title: API Reference
keywords: {"detail": "No backup found for customer 'acme' on date '2026-01-01'"}

  "username": "jsmith",

pages.content: ESC Help API Reference pullDB provides a RESTful API for programmatic access. The API uses JSON for request and response bodies. Most users will use the CLI, but the API is available for integration into other tools. http://pulldb-server:8080/api Replace with your actual pullDB server hostname. Default port is 8080. pullDB uses HMAC-signed requests for secure authentication. All API requests must include signature headers. HMAC Signed Authentication (CLI/Programmatic) CLI tools and programmatic clients must sign every request with HMAC-SHA256. Required headers: : Your API key identifier : ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (e.g., ) : HMAC-SHA256 signature (hex-encoded) curl with HMAC signature $ curl -H "X-API-Key: $PULLDB_API_KEY" \ -H "X-Timestamp: 2026-01-03T15:42:00Z" \ -H "X-Signature: a1b2c3d4e5f6..." \ http://pulldb-server:8080/api/jobs Signature Computation The signature is computed over a canonical string: Where is the SHA256 hash of the request body (or empty string for GET). Session Coo

pages.title: CLI Reference
pages.content: ESC Help CLI Reference Complete reference for pullDB command-line tools. The command is for end users to submit and monitor restore jobs. The command is for system administrators. Quick Reference # Submit a restore $ pulldb restore <customer> # Check your last job status $ pulldb status # Stream events in real-time $ pulldb status --rt # Cancel a job $ pulldb cancel <job_id> Submit a restore job to download a backup from S3 and restore it to your development database. User Command Parameters Parameter Required Default Description <customer> Yes* - Customer ID (positional, lowercase letters only) qatemplate Yes* - Restore QA template instead of customer dbhost= No localhost Target database host suffix= No - Suffix for target database (1-3 lowercase letters) date= No Latest Specific backup date (YYYY-MM-DD) s3env= No prod S3 environment (staging|prod|both) overwrite No false Overwrite existing target database * Either or is required, but not both. Username is auto-detected from your SSH

pages.title: Job Lifecycle
pages.content: ESC Help Concepts Job Lifecycle Understanding how restore jobs move through the system from submission to completion. This knowledge helps you monitor progress, diagnose issues, and understand what's happening behind the scenes. When you submit a restore job, it goes through a series of states and phases . States represent the overall job status (queued, running, complete), while phases describe what the worker is doing during the running state. stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> QUEUED: Submit job QUEUED --> RUNNING: Worker picks up RUNNING --> COMPLETE: Success RUNNING --> FAILED: Error QUEUED --> CANCELED: User cancels RUNNING --> CANCELED: User cancels COMPLETE --> [*] FAILED --> [*] CANCELED --> [*] QUEUED ⏳ Job is waiting for a worker to pick it up. Jobs are processed in FIFO order. Duration: Typically seconds RUNNING ⚙️ A worker is actively processing this job. See phases for what happens during this state. Duration: 2-15 minutes typical COMPLETE ✅ Job finished successfully. Your database

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.content: ESC Help Getting Started Get from zero to your first restored database in under 5 minutes. This guide covers everything you need to start using pullDB. ~5 minutes 1 Before you start, make sure you have: Access to a machine with installed A valid username registered in the system Knowledge of at least one customer ID with available backups Not sure about your username? Contact your team admin or check with whoever manages your pullDB installation. 2 List backups for a customer to see what's available: List Backups $ pulldb list acme Backups for 'acme': DATE TIME (UTC) SIZE ENV FILENAME ---------- ---------- ------ ---- ---------------------------------- 2026-01-02 08:37:36 2.3 GB prod daily_mydumper_acme_2026-01-02... 2026-01-01 08:42:29 2.3 GB prod daily_mydumper_acme_2026-01-01... 2025-12-31 08:40:46 2.2 GB prod daily_mydumper_acme_2025-12-31... 3 backup(s) found. Restore with: pulldb restore acme Tip: The highlighted date is the most recent backup. Use to restore a specific version.

pages.title: Troubleshooting
pages.content: ESC Help Troubleshooting Common issues and their solutions. Can't find what you're looking for? Use the search or check the CLI reference . Start here to quickly identify your issue: 📥 Download Failed S3, network, or permission errors 🔄 Restore Failed myloader, MySQL, or data errors ⏳ Job Stuck Not progressing or queued too long 🔐 Permission Denied Access or authentication issues ❌ "Access Denied" when downloading from S3 Symptoms: Job fails in phase with S3 access error. Causes: AWS credentials expired or missing IAM role doesn't have S3 read permissions Wrong S3 bucket or path configured Solutions: Check your AWS credentials are current: $ aws sts get-caller-identity Verify S3 access: $ aws s3 ls s3://your-bucket/backups/ Contact your admin to verify IAM permissions ❌ "Backup not found" error Symptoms: Job fails immediately with "backup not found" message. Causes: Customer ID misspelled Backup doesn't exist for requested date Backup was deleted or moved Solutions: Verify available ba

pages.title: Admin Panel
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Admin Panel The Admin Panel provides system-wide configuration and management capabilities. Only users with the Admin role can access these features. Admin Access Required This page documents features only available to Admin users. If you don't see the Admin menu item, contact your system administrator to request access. The Admin Panel is your central hub for managing the pullDB system. It's organized into tabs for different administrative functions. Users Create, edit, and manage user accounts. Hosts Configure MySQL hosts for restores. API Keys View and manage all system API keys. Settings System-wide configuration options. The Users tab lets you manage all user accounts in the system. User List The user table displays all accounts with their key properties: Column Description Unique login identifier User, Manager, or Admin Active or Disabled Number of authorized hosts Most recent authentication Edit, Reset Password, Disable Creating Users Click "Create User" to add a

pages.title: Dashboard
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Dashboard The Dashboard is your home base in pullDB. It provides an at-a-glance view of your jobs, system status, and quick actions tailored to your role. What you see on the Dashboard depends on your role. Users see their own job activity, Managers see system-wide metrics, and Admins get full access to administration panels. Role Dashboard Features Recent jobs, quick restore, job stats for your account All user features + system-wide job overview, Manager View link All Manager features + admin panel, system health, user management The standard user dashboard focuses on your personal job activity and provides quick access to common actions. Recent Jobs Panel Shows your 5 most recent restore jobs with current status. Each job row displays: Customer ID — The target customer database Status Badge — Current job state (Queued, Running, Complete, Failed) Submitted Time — When you submitted the job Duration — How long the job took (or is taking) Actions — View details, cancel
pages.preview: ESC Help Web UI Dashboard The Dashboard is your home base in pullDB. It provides an at-a-glance view of your jobs, system status, and quick actions tailored to your role. What you see on the Dashboard...

pages.title: Web UI Overview
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Overview The pullDB Web UI provides a modern, browser-based interface for managing database restores. Submit jobs, monitor progress, manage API keys, and administer the system—all from your browser. The Web UI offers the same functionality as the CLI but with a visual interface that makes it easy to monitor multiple jobs, browse backup history, and manage your account settings. Dashboard At-a-glance view of your jobs, system status, and quick actions. Restore Wizard Step-by-step guided workflow for submitting restore jobs. Job Monitor Real-time progress tracking with detailed phase information. Admin Panel User management, host configuration, and system settings. The Web UI is served on port by default. Access it via your browser: Browser URL http://your-server:8000 Authentication The Web UI supports two authentication methods: Username & Password Log in with your pullDB username and password. This creates a session cookie valid for 24 hours. API Key Use an API key in t

pages.title: Jobs Management
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Jobs Management The Jobs page lets you view, monitor, and manage your restore jobs. Track progress in real-time, view detailed logs, and take actions like canceling or retrying jobs. The Jobs page provides a comprehensive view of all your restore jobs. You can see active jobs updating in real-time, browse job history, and drill down into details. Job List Sortable, filterable table of all jobs. Search & Filter Find jobs by customer, status, or date. Real-Time Updates Live progress for running jobs. Detailed Logs Full execution logs for each job. The main jobs table displays all your jobs with key information at a glance. Table Columns Column Description Sortable Unique identifier for the job Yes Customer ID being restored Yes Current job state with badge Yes Current processing phase (if running) No When the job was submitted Yes Total time (or elapsed time if running) Yes View details, cancel, retry buttons No Pagination By default, 25 jobs are shown per page. Use the p

pages.title: Profile & API Keys
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Profile & API Keys Manage your account settings, change your password, and create API keys for programmatic access to pullDB from scripts and automation tools. The Profile page is your personal settings area. Here you can view your account information, change your password, and manage API keys for each host you have access to. Account Info View your username, role, and account status. Password Change your password securely. API Keys Create and manage per-host API keys. The profile card shows your current account information. Field Description Your unique login identifier (cannot be changed) Your permission level: User, Manager, or Admin Account status: Active or Disabled When your account was created Your most recent login timestamp List of hosts you can restore to To change your password, use the "Change Password" form in the profile page. Requirements Current Password — You must enter your current password to verify your identity Minimum Length — New password must be

pages.title: Restore Workflow
pages.content: ESC Help Web UI Restore Workflow The restore page provides a guided wizard for submitting database restore jobs. Follow the steps to select a customer, choose a backup, configure options, and submit. ~2 minutes to submit The restore workflow is a 4-step wizard that guides you through submitting a restore job. Each step validates your input before allowing you to proceed. 1 Customer 2 Backup 3 Options 4 Confirm Enter the customer ID whose database you want to restore. The system will verify that backups exist for this customer. Customer ID Field Auto-complete — Start typing to see matching customer IDs Validation — The system checks if backups exist before proceeding Recent Customers — Quick links to customers you've restored recently Case Sensitivity Customer IDs are case-sensitive. Make sure to enter them exactly as they appear in your backup system. What Happens When you enter a customer ID and click "Next", the system queries S3 for available backups. If no backups are found, you'll
